publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-11-01 00:18:50 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Chiefly incoming correspondence from publishers, agents, friends, and readers, as well as materials relating to Vonnegut's family and personal life from the period he lived in Barnstable, MA (1950s-1970s). Highlights also include his notebooks from his time as a graduate student in anthropology at the University of Chicago from 1945 to 1947 after serving in World War II; several manuscript notes or typescripts of plays, essays, speeches and screenplays; and scrapbooks Vonnegut compiled while living in upstate New York 1947-1950, preserving his work as a publicist for the General Electric Company.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Kurt Vonnegut papers document Kurt Vonnegut's work as a writer and his personal life between the 1950s and 1970s. Items includes manuscript drafts, notebooks, letters and material from Vonneguts time with the General Electric Company.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Kurt Vonnegut was born in Indianapolis on November 11, 1922. He was an American novelist best known for such works as Slaughterhouse-five, Cat's cradle, and God bless you, Mr. Rosewater. He died in Manhattan on April 11, 2007.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Kurt Vonnegut papers, #8789. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily University of Chicago class notebooks and early literary notes.

did.unittitle: Notes for Welcome to the Monkey House

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Vonnegut worked as a night reporter for the City News Bureau of Chicago while attending the University of Chicago.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Through the GI Bill, Vonnegut studied anthropology at the University of Chicago from 1945-1947. He received his graduate degree in anthropology 25 years after he left, when the University accepted his novel Cat's Cradle in lieu of his master's thesis in 1971.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder contains outlines of courses to be taken in anthropology to qualify for a degree and letter from the University about what it would take for him to complete his masters without going back to school.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vonnegut's course notebooks. Subjects include archeaology, anthropology, and history. Books also include notes for City News reporting and doodles. Notebook in folder 20 contains notes on Dresden.

did.unittitle: Notes on Billy Graham, society, life, and soldiering

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Vonnegut worked as a technical writer, then publicist, for the company's Schenectady, New York, research laboratory from 1947 until 1951.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chiefly materials documenting Vonnegut's time at General Atlantic.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes published GE scrapbook of history inscribed to Vonnegut by former GE president Charles Wilson.

did.unittitle: Camps General Electric Research and Engineering Program: program and script

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescripts and correspondence relating to Kurt's (unpublished?) story Dateline, Spruce Falls, along with rejection note from Readers Digest.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescripts, drafts, contracts, notes, and correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts of Vonnegut's commencement address.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescripts, notes, and itineraries regarding Vonnegut's trip to Biafra.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescripts for the essay "The Mysterious Madame Blavatsky".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescripts of article for McCall's magazine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed paragraph of memory about why Vonnegut went to Cornell.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily letters from agents. Includes publisher's letter on Cat's Cradle; letter to Kurt offering to return the advance on Slaughterhouse 5, Feb 1957.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts, notes, correspondence, and clippings on Cape Cod serial killer Antone Charles "Tony" Costa for the essay "There's a Maniac Loose Out There"

did.unittitle: God Bless You, Mr. Rosewater contract

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript for Herman Hesse and the American Young.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous notes, typescripts (mostly incomplete), and correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Treatment for an original screenplay.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Treatment cover letter addressed to Max Wilkinson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains playbills, press, and invitation list.

did.unittitle: The Rover Boys in the American Southwest K.V.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescripts for "Tornado" and "Uncle Upstairs".

did.unittitle: "Vonnegut's War: And So It Goes" (article in Boston After Dark)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Review of Slaughterhouse Five.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript review of Going All the Way by Dan Wakefield.

did.unittitle: War Claims Commission and veteran's info. (bonus)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Records regarding compensation granted to Vonnegut as a former prisioner of war.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Archivist supplied title. Original box labelled "Early Writing- Cape Cod, rejection slips".

did.unittitle: Trip to England-- Kurt and Jane

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous theatre programs, correspondence, postcards, menus, and reciepts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chiefly newspapers and clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Memorial booklet and photograph.

did.unittitle: Sub-subseries IIa.1. KV writing correspondence. Volume 1. Closed June 1953

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chiefly incoming letters from the Author's Guild of America.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder includes extensive incoming correspondence from Knox Burger, Kurt's friend and first publisher; Vonnegut's admittance letter to the University of Chicago.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chiefly incoming correspondence from Kenneth Littauer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chiefly letters from Kenneth Litteurer and Max Wilkinson at Littauer and Wilkinson, Inc. Examples include letter saying that Cat's Cradle is being considered by the New Yorker, followed by a letter of rejection by New Yorker; letter confirming the publication of Cat's Cradle and forwarding Kurt's share of the $1,000 proceeds, letter enclosing a contract with Houghton Mifflin for Sirens of Titan, suggesting that he ask for not more than $20k but not less than $10k in compensation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from the Department of the Air Force, 24 Feb 1956 re: Vonnegut's request for information concerning details of the Dresden raids.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from Knox Burger.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes note written by Vonnegut as a child.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter of reference for Vonnegut from GE dated 1951.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter to Vonnegut from Norman Mailer.

did.unittitle: Sub-subseries IIa.3. Random correspondence from miscellaneous years (1960)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chiefly professional correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter to Vonnegut Kurt from Ray Bradbury (3/28/59) and letter to Bradbury from Knox Burger (undated)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Norman Mailer (February 18, 1960), and typed pages for "Three Cases of Bitchery Cured by Durance Vile, M.D. (pseudonym of Kurt Vonnegut, Jr.)."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes typewritten poem by Vonnegut to Robert (B. Ruthman).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes typewritten essay by Vonnegut on Cape Cod theatre, titled "Slightly Salty" and SAAB dealership records.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A wide range of publishing correspondence from multiple agents and reps from publishing houses, from readers, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Vonnegut to reviewer/friend Ed (Darling?).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Harlan Ellison.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes telegram from Harlan Ellison.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Harlan Ellison

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from William F. Buckley, Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Gene Shalit and Vonnegut's screen treatment for God Bless You, Mr. Rosewater.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from E.L. Doctorow.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes mimeograph invitation to a party for Allen Ginsberg.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from John Irving.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes essay Vonnegut wrote upon agent Kenneth Littauer's death.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Hugh Hefner.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from E.L. Doctorow.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from John Irving.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copy of letter from Vonnegut to the draft board about his son's conscientious objector status and citing his own military service.

did.unittitle: Sub-subseries IIa.10. Letters from many years, kicking around and finally filed in 1969

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Vonnegut to agents pitching an article about tornadoes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correpondence between Vonnegut and his agents regarding his progress on God Bless You, Mr. Rosewater and rejection by publishers of Cat's Cradle and Nation of Two (Mother Night).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes travel documents and receipts for Vonnegut's trip to Dresden.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Harlan Ellison praising Vonnegut for Slaughterhouse Five.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from John Irving.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes contact sheet of pictures taken of Vonnegut at Slaughterhouse Five book signing at the Paperback Booksmith, Boston, April 11, 1969.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes telegram from "Kilgore Trout" and Feb. 28, 1969 letter from "Colonel" Vonnegut served in WWII with.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from Rex Stout inviting Vonnegut to join the Author's Guild.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Ray Bradbury.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs from a theatrical performance of God Bless You, Mr. Rosewater.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Harlan Ellison.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Abbie Hoffman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from John Irving.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes invitation to Apollo 11 launch.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from Harlan Ellison.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from John Irving.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from John Irving.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from Anne Sexton.

did.unittitle: Sub-subseries IIa.18. Happy Birthday, Wanda June (October 1970-March 1971)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chiefly newspaper clippings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series arranged in chronological order, with files in alphabetical order by sender.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chiefly personal letters, holiday cards, postcards, and invitations addressed to Jane, Kurt, and their children, along with clippings and playbills documenting the Vonneguts' participation in local Cape Cod theatrical groups.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Archivist supplied title. Materials found loose in original letterbox.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Archivist supplied title. Materials found loose in original letterbox.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chiefly personal letters, postcards, and invitations to Kurt and Jane Vonnegut.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Archivist supplied title. Materials found loose in original letterbox.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes envelope to G.B. Jeffrey with humorous drawing by Pfc. Kurt Vonnegut, 1940.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes thank you note from Ted Kennedy.
